primary_reason: This network simulation of critical-care transfers compares infection-control targeting strategies for dissemination of resistant hospital organisms.
This paper develops an agent-based representation of gut host-pathogen interactions and uses it to test mechanisms and generate experimental hypotheses for gut-derived sepsis.
This study establishes surgical outcome benchmarks for distal pancreatectomy without an infectious-disease outcome or a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This simulation investigates inherited cardiomyopathy physiology and does not provide a concrete infectious-disease application or transfer route.
This simulation study evaluates marginal structural Cox estimators and proposes normalized inverse-probability weights for time-varying confounding.
This agent-based farm model synthesizes the Toxoplasma gondii life cycle to examine transmission pathways and control strategies.
This agent-based sexual-network simulation examines how heterogeneous sexual motivation affects HIV acquisition risk and intervention targeting.
This quality-indicator comparison concerns head and neck cancer surgery rather than an infectious-disease application or transferable infectious method.
This stochastic foot-and-mouth disease model compares alternative passive-surveillance network structures for outbreak detection and control.
This comment summarizes another study's intensive-care quality-improvement trial rather than presenting sufficient original methodological content.
This agent-based neonatal-gut model tests how oxidative stress, bacteria, and mucus-barrier failure can jointly produce necrotizing enterocolitis.
This field study evaluates E. coli as a waterborne-pathogen indicator and develops reference-site-based environmental pathogen benchmarks.
This HIV cohort analysis uses directed acyclic graphs and tracked losses to compare approaches for correcting loss-to-follow-up bias.
This multicentre prediction-model validation concerns intensive-care delirium without an infectious-disease outcome or concrete transfer route.
This HIV policy analysis constructs regional and global antiretroviral price benchmarks and estimates treatment gains under alternative procurement prices.
This calibrated agent-based malaria model links within-host immunity and antigenic variation to transmission in heterogeneous communities.
This decision model projects the lifetime health and economic effects of HIV pre-exposure prophylaxis under alternative efficacy and incidence assumptions.
This simulation study compares finite-sample bias, precision, and coverage of crude, adjusted, and inverse-probability-weighted marginal structural Cox models.
This review critically describes risk-adjustment methods and challenges for benchmarking hospital antimicrobial drug use.
This agent-based model compares phagocyte movement and communication strategies for clearing opportunistic fungal spores.
This methods report develops procedures for assigning schools, workplaces, and transit attributes to a synthetic population for infectious-disease agent-based models.
This review and multicentre benchmark assessment specifies preanalytical blood-culture collection indicators and identifies measurement deficiencies.
This real-data and simulated surveillance analysis quantifies how participation duration and selection bias affect CLABSI benchmark validity.
This benchmarking evaluation addresses coronary bypass surgery outcomes without an infectious-disease application or concrete infectious transfer route.
This molecular-dynamics study evaluates substrate transport mechanisms in a Pseudomonas aeruginosa channel relevant to antimicrobial resistance and drug development.
This hybrid agent-based and phylogenetic analysis reconstructs HIV transmission between injecting-drug-use and heterosexual risk groups in Latvia.
This multicentre study shows that trauma-centre VAP benchmarks depend materially on diagnostic definitions, exclusions, and reporting practices.
This hospital report describes sharps-injury incidence and staff risk factors using a standard patient-day rate without evaluating a transferable measurement design.
This agent-based parasite-growth model quantifies stochastic error and sample-size limits in comparative gyrodactylid infection experiments.
This pharmacokinetic dosing simulation targets chemotherapy-induced neutropenia rather than an infectious-disease outcome or transferable infectious method.
This systematic synthesis develops pooled control limits and sample-size guidance for benchmarking inappropriate empirical antibiotic treatment.
This global HIV review combines epidemiologic evidence with agent-based transmission modelling to identify network and biological drivers of MSM epidemics.
This stochastic plant-pathogen simulation evaluates virulence evolution and the risk of super-races under perennial cultivar mixtures.
This paper describes automated ICU-associated-infection surveillance and concise reporting tools designed to support benchmarking with minimal staff effort.
This letter has no supplied abstract or substantive methodological content beyond its title.
This EuroSIDA analysis operationalizes four HIV-care indicators to compare guideline adherence and virological performance across regions.
This hospital patient-experience survey is not an infectious-disease study, and its incidental infection-control item supplies no concrete transfer route.
This molecular simulation studies biocide-induced lipid-bilayer damage without an infectious-disease epidemiologic application or concrete transferable design.
This multiway simulation evaluates how HPV vaccine coverage, effectiveness, and screening patterns affect cervical-disease equity.
This hybrid model examines preventative cancer vaccination and tumour immunity rather than an infectious-disease application or concrete transfer route.
This stochastic MRSA transmission model compares patient and healthcare-worker decolonization with isolation in endemic hospitals.
This comment and letter provides no supplied abstract or sufficient original methodological content.
This cellular-automaton and agent-based prototype simulates HPV16 growth and persistence across cervical lesion stages.
This mathematical policy model compares the costs and HIV-prevention effects of treatment-as-prevention, circumcision, and conventional antiretroviral treatment.
This agent-based epithelial host-pathogen model tests mechanisms by which virulent Pseudomonas can cause postoperative anastomotic leak.
This paper updates and benchmarks an in-silico B-cell epitope prediction method while demonstrating how benchmark definitions alter measured performance.
This national audit identifies definitional and data-collection variation that makes surgical-site-infection surveillance rates unsuitable for benchmarking.
This study evaluates how inclusion of perioperative variables in standardized infection-ratio models can distort surgical-site-infection benchmarking.
This mechanistic model fitted to African survey data estimates within-couple and extra-couple contributions to heterosexual HIV transmission.
This stochastic dairy-herd model evaluates whether imperfect vaccination can prevent Mycobacterium avium paratuberculosis invasion and identifies influential transmission parameters.
This large screening analysis derives CIN3-plus risk thresholds to guide consistent management of HPV and Pap cotesting results.
This influenza-like-illness agent-based simulation evaluates public wait-time information as a policy for balancing emergency-department demand during outbreaks.
This paper develops a data-fusion approach that integrates demographic, mobile-phone, and Bluetooth-contact data to improve respiratory-infection agent-based models.
This preliminary agent-based STI model evaluates the modelling framework's ability to compare condom use, network structure, testing, and behavioural interventions.
This exposure-response benchmark-dose analysis concerns perfluorinated compounds and vaccine antibody titres without a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This vaccine-safety simulation compares self-controlled case-series corrections for bias when an adverse event contraindicates subsequent exposure.
This mechanistic within-host malaria model estimates human infectiousness to mosquitoes and the human component of transmission potential.
This comment has no supplied abstract or sufficient methodological, diagnostic, or guidance content.
This hybrid model concerns pressure-ulcer formation after spinal-cord injury rather than an infectious-disease application or concrete transfer route.
This agent-based model investigates breast-cancer oncogenesis without an infectious-disease outcome or concrete infectious transfer route.
This study proposes a mechanistic model linking fungal wing invasion, altered physiological homeostasis, and mortality in white-nose syndrome in bats.
This metagenomic genome reconstruction identifies mechanistic metabolic and resistance adaptations supporting persistence of an opportunistic pathogen in cystic-fibrosis lungs.
This county-scale influenza agent-based model estimates workplace transmission reductions from paid sick days and influenza-specific leave policies.
This paper explains methodological and ethical challenges in evaluating the cost-effectiveness of new tuberculosis diagnostics among people living with HIV.
This agent-based model evaluates how provider dismissal policies can cluster vaccine-hesitant families and create local susceptibility pockets.
This multicentre evaluation shows that product-specific hand-rub doses bias volume-based hand-hygiene compliance benchmarking.
This agent-based animal-to-human brucellosis model evaluates livestock control strategies and their effects on human incidence in Georgia.
This tuberculosis simulation combines notification data, patient delay, and incubation distributions to test whether winter transmission explains observed seasonality.
This statewide cohort analysis quantifies missed surgical-site infections and altered hospital rankings when surveillance excludes readmissions elsewhere.
This study proposes hierarchical and Bayesian methods for risk-adjusted regional healthcare-associated-infection benchmarking and outlier identification.
This concept paper proposes surgical-service quality metrics and only mentions infectious diseases as part of broader global-health context.
This rainfall-driven chikungunya agent-based model evaluates spatial network structure, asymptomatic movement, and travel restrictions during outbreaks.
This paper develops a diversity index for selecting dose-response model spaces for model-averaged benchmark and infectious-dose estimation.
This agent-based HIV model examines feedback between risk perception, sexual behaviour, and transmission dynamics.
This review explains risk adjustment, benchmark selection, and interpretation challenges for local healthcare-associated-infection surveillance data.
This study extends self-controlled case series methods for underreported, time-varying vaccine-safety reports and evaluates their operating performance by simulation.
This GIS-informed agent-based model examines how landscape heterogeneity and host movement shape parasite transmission and validates simulated dispersal against macaque gene-flow data.
This mechanistic mathematical model quantifies IL-15 receptor signaling and natural-killer-cell proliferation, providing a within-host framework for pathogen-defense dynamics.
This study implements a stakeholder-informed, probabilistic measurement and benchmarking process for evaluating HPV vaccination-service quality.
This quantitative microbial risk assessment evaluates pathogen risks from rainwater-irrigated produce and critically assesses the annual infection-risk benchmark used in guidance.
This study develops and validates an epigenetic biomarker for endometrial cancer rather than an infectious-disease method with a concrete transfer route.
This simulation study compares bias-correction approaches for self-controlled case series analyses of rare vaccine adverse events.
This retrospective analysis reports treatment-response predictors for HIV-HCV coinfected patients without developing or evaluating a transferable design or method.
This study describes predictors and causes of post-stroke readmission, with infection only one incidental readmission cause.
This stochastic agent-based HIV transmission model compares PrEP targeting strategies through population-level cost-effectiveness simulations.
This paper proposes a semantic-web and multi-agent framework for integrating heterogeneous data and services in epidemic-management decision support.
This age-structured transmission model projects direct and herd-protection effects of childhood influenza vaccination strategies in Germany.
This paper develops and benchmarks a distributed platform for global-scale agent-based epidemic simulations, including its communication and synchronization design.
This calibrated agent-based tuberculosis model estimates how household contact tracing and preventive therapy affect incidence under differing household-transmission assumptions.
This simulation concerns eradication of an invasive agricultural insect pest rather than pathogen transmission or an infectious-disease application.
This study develops and internally assesses a practical clinical prediction score for recurrent Clostridium difficile infection from phase III trial data.
This systematic review evaluates implementation research on school wellness policies for childhood obesity rather than infectious-disease outcomes.
This wear-simulator experiment assesses implant mechanics after vancomycin powder exposure without measuring infection prevention or infectious-disease dynamics.
This paper develops and evaluates a low-complexity calibration method that maps field reproduction-number estimates to pandemic-influenza agent-based simulations.
This study evaluates receiver-operating-characteristic analysis for predicting Giardia and Cryptosporidium occurrence from water-quality indicators and deriving local criteria.
This agent-based wound model simulates host-cell and bacterial-virulence interactions to investigate mechanisms of surgical-site infection.
This work demonstrates agent-based Monte Carlo simulation as a method for designing and interpreting HIV prevention randomized trials.
This fine-grained Chicago agent-based model reproduces community-associated MRSA trends and identifies colonized people and households as intervention targets.
This agent-based incarceration model borrows an SIS analogy but studies social influence on imprisonment rather than an infectious-disease outcome.
This temperature-sensitive agent-based model examines Schistosoma mansoni transmission, snail dynamics, and implications for control under water warming.
This in-vitro blood-pump study measures hemocompatibility outcomes, while infection is only a contextual device complication.
This study derives somatic-cell-count benchmarks for monitoring dairy-herd intramammary infection dynamics and evaluates associated risk factors.
This stochastic HIV dynamics model estimates latent-reservoir reductions needed to avoid viral rebound and informs cure-trial design.
This pharmaceutical formulation study characterizes an intravaginal anti-HIV delivery device rather than an infectious-disease epidemiologic method or design.
This calibrated hospital agent-based model compares Clostridium difficile control strategies while representing patient progression, staff, visitors, and environmental transmission.
This agent-based oral-biofilm model identifies tipping points in cariogenic bacterial composition and evaluates non-lethal intervention mechanisms.
This simulation compares trigger and transmission mechanisms for Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae outbreaks in finishing pigs.
This agent-based model represents intestinal ecology and bile-acid mechanisms to simulate Clostridium difficile infection and fecal microbial transplant effects.
This agent-based model studies social norms, diet, and childhood obesity rather than an infectious-disease outcome.
This systems model investigates general cellular autophagy dynamics rather than a specified infectious-disease process.
This spatial individual-based model tests how host aggregation and disease avoidance alter lobster viral outbreak intensity and persistence.
This study derives a general postoperative-morbidity benchmark for pancreatoduodenectomy rather than an infectious-disease-specific method.
This benchmarking study evaluates cancer mutation-prediction algorithms without an infectious-disease application or concrete transfer route.
This spatial agent-based model predicts Toxoplasma environmental contamination and rodent infection risk under alternative rural landscape configurations.
This simulation study evaluates missing-data corrections for marginal structural models, including a clinical-cohort scenario relevant to time-dependent confounding.
This spatiotemporal alveolus agent-based model tests macrophage migration mechanisms during early Aspergillus fumigatus infection.
This paper develops a life-cycle agent-based Anopheles model and uses hypothetical vector-control interventions to evaluate impacts on infectious mosquitoes.
This study develops a smoothed age-dependent mixture model for HPV seroprevalence that avoids cut-off-based serostatus misclassification.
This agent-based model evaluates combinations of testing, treatment, PrEP, and behavioral interventions for HIV prevention among South African MSM.
This review explains proposed payment rules and benchmark-design methodologies for monitoring surgical infections.
This comparison reports discrepancies between general surgical-outcome databases without developing an infectious-disease surveillance or analytic method.
This genomic analysis produces a historical Shigella flexneri reference genome and compares antimicrobial-resistance and virulence changes across a century.
This pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic Monte Carlo simulation compares antibiotic target attainment under delayed or missed dosing for respiratory pathogens.
This simulation study compares sequential monitoring and confounder-control methods for rare-event observational vaccine-safety surveillance.
This policy analysis proposes domestic HIV-financing metrics, applies future spending scenarios across 12 countries, and estimates resulting funding gaps.
This paper introduces a stable matrix-based method for oxygen diffusion within an agent-based tuberculosis granuloma model.
This spatially explicit Ebola model calibrated by Markov chain Monte Carlo estimates transmission settings and evaluates treatment units, safe burials, and protection kits.
This agent-based influenza model represents behavior-change mechanisms to estimate effects of vaccination, distancing, and education on outbreak control.
This prospective surveillance study benchmarks healthcare-worker percutaneous injuries using a standardized reporting system for blood-borne pathogen exposure risk.
This paper develops an inflammation imaging tracer without evaluating an infectious disease or specifying a concrete infectious-disease application.
This tutorial substantively explains agent-based modeling for economic evaluation and supplies an infectious-disease screening case study and model resource.
This simulation combines influenza incidence, vaccine effectiveness, and Guillain-Barré risks to estimate the vaccine's net absolute safety effect.
This paper develops copula-based selection models to relax bivariate-normality assumptions and evaluates HIV-prevalence estimation by simulation and DHS application.
This study develops solvable and mechanistic models of HIV immune-escape evolution to explain sequence scaling laws and inform vaccine design.
This paper develops a hybrid network-patch approach that extends directly transmitted disease agent-based models to mosquito-borne transmission.
This case study describes benchmarking educational resources and implementation recommendations for community health-worker e-learning without evaluating a transferable epidemiologic method.
This national NSQIP comparison benchmarks early surgical-site infection after tissue-expander breast reconstruction and assesses patient and procedural risk factors.
This high-performance enteric-tissue model demonstrates pouchitis pathogenesis without establishing an infectious-disease outcome or transmission application.
This agent-based HIV model represents partnership behaviors among Taiwanese homosexual men and calibrates simulated epidemic curves to reported cases.
This qualitative study describes healthcare professionals' social representations of HIV/AIDS without developing or assessing a transferable method.
This paper presents a general stochastic agent-based framework for pathogens in dynamic multi-relational social networks and illustrates it with HIV among injection-drug users.
This simulation study evaluates the validity and power of randomized and stepped-wedge Ebola vaccine trial designs under spatiotemporally heterogeneous incidence.
This prospective audit reports complication rates for minimally invasive foot surgery, with only one superficial infection and no transferable infectious-disease method.
This California measles agent-based simulation evaluates vaccination coverage, immunity clustering, tracing probability, and response delays in outbreak prevention.
This conference abstract duplicates the national tissue-expander reconstruction surgical-site infection benchmarking study retained as PMID:25798391.
This general hospital-management chapter explains benchmarking practices without an infectious-disease application beyond incidental examples.
This pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic simulation compares antibiotic target attainment after de-escalation in critically ill patients and tests alternative dosing strategies.
This benchmarking study compares virtual-screening strategies using an unbiased Toll-like-receptor-8 dataset to identify vaccine-adjuvant candidates.
This simulation study evaluates an improved Farrington algorithm for outbreak detection from slaughterhouse condemnation data used in animal-health syndromic surveillance.
This paper evaluates data-layout, sorting, and parallelization strategies that substantially improve computational performance of infectious-disease agent-based transmission models.
